examples: In a head-to-head contest between a moral relativist and a fundamentalist, who wins?
One might, for example, be an Absolutist about liberal and a Relativist about vocational education.
Einstein, however, was not truly a relativist, even though that is how he was interpreted by many, including some whose disdain was tinged by anti-Semitism.
As a self-taught politician, he had come to know what was right and what was wrong, and said so in terms that shocked an increasingly relativist world.
